newsSinceJun6: June 7: UBTECH announced cumulative pre-orders for its UWORLD/YouWorld full-size ultra-bionic humanoid robot (U1) exceeded 2,110 units within six days of JD.com pre-sale launch (3,000 yuan deposit, ~$30k expected price, deliveries no later than Sept 15, official unveiling June 30). Stock popped >6% intraday June 8 on the news; June 9 is -0.85% drift. Also: strategic cooperation agreement with Hitachi (China) entering substantive validation phase. No new earnings, M&A, or regulatory items.

sources.label: Futu News - UBTECH rose on hyper-bionic humanoid pre-sales, 2,110+ orders in six days
Gasgoo - UBTECH ultra-bionic humanoid pre-orders surpass 2,110 units
Pandaily - UBTECH biomimetic humanoid surpasses 1,000 pre-orders in 3 days (pricing, delivery timeline)
Interesting Engineering - UBTECH life-like humanoid companion details ($30k, specs)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already promoted the name (22 -> 16) on commercialization and under-ownership, and the June 7 pre-order news drove a +6% pop on June 8 that partly consumed the fresh asymmetry; today is -0.85% drift with no new June 9 information, so hold rank with a small positive score drift for the genuinely new consumer-demand datapoint.
verdicts.bull: 2,110+ paid-deposit pre-orders in six days for a ~$30k consumer humanoid is real demand validation in a second segment beyond Walker S2 industrial orders (800M+ yuan), and YTD is still -11% on a $7.1B cap, leaving rerating room into the June 30 U1 unveiling.
verdicts.neutral: Pre-orders carry only a 3,000 yuan refundable deposit, so conversion to ~$63M of revenue is unproven; the news is already reflected in the June 8 pop and the name just moved up six rank slots yesterday.
verdicts.bear: This is the consensus China humanoid ticker - JD.com retail buzz increases crowding rather than hidden alpha, gross margins on consumer companion bots are unproven, and a soft June 30 launch or weak deposit-to-sale conversion would unwind the pop.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 30 official U1 launch and first deposit-to-purchase conversion data; deliveries due by Sept 15.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Pre-order conversion disappoints or the launch underwhelms, exposing the name as a crowded retail-driven humanoid proxy rather than a commercialization story.
